Horizon: An American Saga Chapter 1 (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $29,035,702Details
International Box Office $9,800,000
Worldwide Box Office $38,835,702
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,019,255
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,754,982
Total $3,774,237
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Horizon: An American Saga explores the lure of the Old West and how it was won — and lost — through the blood, sweat and tears of many. Spanning the four years of the Civil War, from 1861 to 1865, the cinematic adventure will take audiences on an emotional journey across a country at war with itself, experienced through the lens of families, friends and foes all attempting to discover what it truly means to be the United States of America.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,052,561 (38.1% of total gross)
Legs:2.63 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:74.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$50,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,334 opening theaters/3,334 max. theaters, 3.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $30,704,418

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 28th, 2024 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: June 27th, 2024 (Wide), released as Horizon: An American Saga Chapter 1 (Горизонт: Американская сага) (Russia (CIS))
June 27th, 2024 (Wide) (Croatia)
June 27th, 2024 (Wide), released as HORIZONT: AMERICKÁ SÁGA (Slovakia)
June 27th, 2024 (Wide), released as Horizont: Americká sága (Czech Republic)
June 28th, 2024 (Wide) (Argentina)
Video Release: July 16th, 2024 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for violence, some nudity and sexuality.
(Rating bulletin 2803 (cert #54559), 10/4/2023)
Running Time: 181 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Directing Yourself, Ensemble, Screenplay Written By the Star
Film festival: Cannes Film Festival 2024
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): Utah, United States
Social setting: The Civil War
Subgenre: Western Drama
Time period setting: 1800s, 1860s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Western
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: New Line Cinema, Territory Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
